description_long: This object is a traditional water well featuring a circular stone base constructed from stacked, gray stone blocks. The well is topped with a pitched, gabled roof made of blue ceramic or slate tiles, supported by four sturdy wooden posts. The roof has a slight upward curve at the edges, giving it an ornamental appearance. The interior of the well is open, with no visible cover or water-drawing mechanism such as a pulley or bucket. The stonework appears robust and functional, while the wooden beams and tiled roof provide shelter from the elements. There are no visible decorative elements or additional protective barriers beyond the main structure.
description: A circular stone water well with a blue-tiled gabled roof supported by wooden posts, lacking a visible cover or water-drawing mechanism.
description_view_0: Front view showing the blue-tiled roof and the circular stone base, with the wooden posts partially visible beneath the roof.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the gabled roof's peak and the intersection of the wooden beams, with the stone base visible below.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the blue-tiled roof and stone base, with no additional features visible.
description_view_3: Angled rear view emphasizing the roof's structure and the supporting wooden beams, with the stone base below.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: The well is surrounded by a circular stone wall rising to about waist height, with a wooden post and gabled roof structure above.
